extra_model_fields.notes: If environment library availability can be changed, regenerate the .xlsx using openpyxl and include it as an alternative artifact to help future preview tooling.
extra_model_fields.validation_goal: Ensure graders can confirm formulas, row-level citations, tie-break rules, and at least one reproducibility check without needing to run binary workbook parsers in the evaluation environment.

planned_changes: Add Evidence_Matrix_values.csv: export of the Evidence_Matrix sheet with evaluated values (one row per record).

Add Evidence_Matrix_formulas.csv: export of the same sheet but with cell text showing the formulas (e.g., show cell contents as formulas), so evaluators can inspect formula logic without parsing the .xlsx.
Add expert_memo_appendix_row_citations.txt: explicit mapping from each input row (R1/R2/R3) to exact sheet and cell references used in calculations (e.g., Evidence_Matrix!A16:D16).

Add reproducibility_check.txt: document one small input change (specify changed field and new value), include before-and-after CSV snapshots showing updated computed outputs, and call out which formulas update accordingly.

Update expert_memo.md: explicitly document handling of R3.machine_capacity_hours=0 (decision rule applied), clarify sign interpretation for O2, and describe tie-break logic (secondary keys or deterministic ordering) with direct formula references.
Update final_message.txt to list added files and request regrading; correct any inconsistent claims about memo preview in grader-facing notes if you produce an internal grader comment file.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifiability gaps (workbook parse/preview limitations and memo -> workbook explicit citations) so graders can fully validate formulas, row-level citations, tie-break logic, and sensitivity analyses without manual binary inspection.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt produced required artifacts and passed deterministic artifact-contract checks, but semantic verification was partially constrained by a preview/parsing gap for the .xlsx and an inconsistency in grader statements about memo preview. The grader reduced the score to 0.75 because cell-level inspection (formulas, explicit row->cell citation mapping, tie-break logic, and sensitivity details) could not be independently validated from the workbook preview. Verifier notes show the expert memo text preview is available and supports many claims, and the trace documents that the workbook was generated via manual OOXML construction with embedded formulas. To reach full credit, the worker should add artifacts and documentation that make the workbook's formulas, row-level citations, and reproducibility checks directly inspectable without requiring manual binary inspection.
